Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.

- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.

  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Agent configuration directory auto-detection logic</title>

  <facts>

    <fact>Agent type detection examines config.agent.command name for keywords: "codex" → ~/.codex, "claude" → ~/.claude, "kiro" → ~/.kiro</fact>

    <fact>detectSkillDir() checks SKILL_DIR env var first, otherwise derives path as ${agentConfigDir}/skills</fact>
    <fact>Path resolution designed to keep AGENT_CONFIG_DIR and SKILL_DIR aligned by default while allowing independent overrides</fact>

  <narrative>The telegram-kiro-bridge implements multi-agent-CLI compatibility through auto-detection logic in src/memory.ts. The detectAgentConfigDir() function resolves the configuration directory using a priority chain: explicit AGENT_CONFIG_DIR env var > agent command name heuristic (inspecting config.agent.command for "codex"/"claude"/"kiro" keywords) > filesystem existence check (~/.kiro then ~/.codex) > default to ~/.kiro. This allows the same bridge code to work with different AI agent CLIs (Kiro, Codex, Claude Code) without hardcoded paths. The detectSkillDir() function follows similar logic, checking SKILL_DIR override first, then deriving it as <agentConfigDir>/skills to maintain alignment. This design enables "the same SKILL.md work on the Kiro box, Codex box, or Claude Code box" as noted in comments, making skills and configurations portable across installations.</narrative>
